overview: The Village of Weston Board of Trustees approved increased park and recreation impact fees, passed several ordinances including mobile food vendor licensing and e-bicycle regulations, and discussed ongoing concerns about the farmers market relocation to Kennedy Park. The board also adjourned the Board of Review to June 15, 2026 and made appointments to the CLIPS committee.

agenda.item: Board of Review called to order and adjourned to June 15, 2026
Board of Trustees meeting called to order, roll call
Public hearing on park and recreation impact fees (Ordinance 26-010 and Resolution 2026-015)
Approval of minutes from previous meetings
Acknowledge reports from boards, committees, and commissions with chair updates
Consent agenda - vouchers and license renewals
Ordinances including mobile food vendor, nuisances, e-bicycles, and transportation funding resolution
Discussion on farmers market location at Kennedy Park

discussions.item: Public Hearing - Park and Recreation Impact Fees
discussions.body: Staff presented a proposed increase to park and recreation impact fees from $300 per single family unit to $761, $250 per duplex to $563, and multifamily to $495, based on the 2022 public facilities needs assessment. Jim Pitel spoke during public comment questioning the justification for the numbers without detailed park maintenance cost data. Trustee Motti supported the fee increase, noting population growth requires more park space. Ordinance 26-010 was approved with motion by Lope Sorrell, second by Zagami, passed unanimously. Resolution 2026-015 was approved with motion by Hermling, second by Clark, passed unanimously.

The board debated amendments to the remote meeting attendance policy. Discussion centered on removing specific justification requirements for remote attendance. Trustee Beck moved to amend the policy to strike sentences requiring health, safety, or welfare justifications, keeping only the requirements to notify officials 12 hours in advance, be on camera, and not participate in closed sessions. The amendment passed with two no votes from Trustees Hermling and Motti. The original motion as amended also passed with the same two dissenting votes.

The president appointed Stephanie Daniels (currently on Finance and HR) and Golden Mua to the CLIPS committee. Motion by Hermling, second by Lopes, approved unanimously. The board discussed having committee applications shared in the packet for transparency. Openings remain on Parks, Finance/HR, and one more on CLIPS.

Chief Finkey explained the ordinance creates a regional reciprocity system where food trucks inspected by one participating municipality (including Marshfield, Wisconsin Rapids, Stevens Point, and others) receive a sticker valid across all participating communities, eliminating redundant inspections. Ordinance 26-008 was approved unanimously.

Board members discussed ongoing concerns about the farmers market's new location at Kennedy Park after losing their previous site to development. Trustee Beck raised safety concerns about cars parking on grass along Alderson and within the park. Renee explained the market has grown from 25 to 53 vendor spots and vendors now appreciate having vehicles with them. The board acknowledged if no suitable location can be found, they may need to suspend the market temporarily. No action was taken; this was discussion only.

The board approved a secondhand article and jewelry dealer license for Scoffield Coin and Hobby LLC at 5703 Jamar Street. Chief Hunt explained these licenses protect against stolen merchandise sales. The motion was amended to require satisfactory background check completion and receipt of occupancy permit, as the building is still under construction. Approved unanimously as amended.

Public Works Director Michael Dolan explained the need for outside engineering consultants ($108,000 estimated) for project oversight, noting difficulty filling an engineering technician position due to limited workforce in central Wisconsin. He mentioned even consulting firms are losing employees to relocation to southern Wisconsin.

publicComment: Jim Pitel of 52 Street spoke during public comment criticizing the board for hiring outside engineering consultants when an engineering technician position remains unfilled, questioning why barriers to filling the position haven't been shared with the board. He also criticized the appointment process for committees, noting Lisa Beck was introduced at a SAFER meeting before being officially appointed. Pitel complained about lack of detailed Kennedy Park information in the packet and called for better accountability from the administrator.
actionItems: Park and recreation impact fees increased effective with Ordinance 26-010 and Resolution 2026-015
Board of Review adjourned to June 15, 2026
Comprehensive Plan Steering Committee meeting scheduled for Monday, June 1st at 6:00 PM (joint meeting with Plan Commission)
Staff to prepare intergovernmental service agreement for Ringle building inspection assistance
Weston Avenue speed limit study to be completed and brought back to board
Committee applications to be included in board packets going forward
Stephanie Daniels and Golden Mua appointed to CLIPS committee
Secondhand dealer license approved for Scoffield Coin and Hobby LLC contingent on background check and occupancy permit
Mobile food vendor regional reciprocity ordinance approved
Remote meeting attendance policy amended to remove specific justification requirements
title: Board of Trustees
